sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Suspicious WebDav Client Execution Via Rundll32.EXE
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects "svchost.exe" spawning "rundll32.exe" with command arguments like C:\windows\system32\davclnt.dll,DavSetCookie. This could be an indicator of exfiltration or use of WebDav to launch code (hosted on WebDav Server) or potentially a sign of exploitation of CVE-2023-23397
sigma_analysis_results.rule_author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)

sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects modification of autostart extensibility point (ASEP) in registry.

sigma_analysis_results.rule_description:
Detects "svchost.exe" spawning "rundll32.exe" with command arguments like "C:\windows\system32\davclnt.dll,DavSetCookie".
This could be an indicator of exfiltration or use of WebDav to launch code (hosted on a WebDav server).
Detects a rundll32 that communicates with public IP addresses
A General detection for files being created in the Windows startup directory. This could be an indicator of persistence.
Detects execution of "rundll32.exe" with a parent process of Explorer.exe. This has been observed by variants of Raspberry Robin, as first reported by Red Canary.

This is often used as a method of persistence.
